title: triticum turgidum subsp. subspontaneum
intro: Triticum turgidum subsp. subspontaneum, also known as wild emmer wheat, is a plant species belonging to the wheat family (Poaceae) and is native to the Middle East and the Mediterranean region. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and protect against cell damage. Consuming wild emmer wheat has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing the risk of chronic diseases, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting healthy digestion. Wild emmer wheat can be prepared as an herbal tea, infused into oil, or added to food products, offering a natural and versatile remedy for various health concerns.
